title: Climate change and urban pressures intensify flood risk on the Gulf of Guinea coast
content: Between 20–22 June 2026, the coastal regions of the countries bordering the Gulf of Guinea, particularly Côte d’Ivoire, Ghana, Togo, and Nigeria, experienced exceptionally widespread and severe flooding following intense and persistent rainfall.
snippet: Between 20–22 June 2026, the coastal regions of the countries bordering the Gulf of Guinea, particularly Côte d’Ivoire, Ghana, Togo, and Nigeria, experienced exceptionally widespread and severe

title: The truncated toolkit: disaster risk finance and the limits of autonomy in the Dutch Caribbean
content: This working paper examines how Aruba, Curaçao and Sint Maarten finance disaster recovery and why they make limited use of modern disaster risk financing instruments despite their high exposure to natural hazards.
snippet: This working paper examines how Aruba, Curaçao and Sint Maarten finance disaster recovery and why they make limited use of modern disaster risk financing instruments despite their high exposure to

title: Cornerstone economic research
content: Cornerstone aims to empower those responsible for making strategic decisions to implement sustainable policies and programmes that overcome the challenges of social and economic exclusion, and inequality.
snippet: Cornerstone aims to empower those responsible for making strategic decisions to implement sustainable policies and programmes that overcome the challenges of social and economic exclusion, and

title: Why disaster risk management needs an offline-first communication layer
content: This author's message to disaster risk management professionals is simple: treat communication as lifeline infrastructure and plan for redundancy, just as we do for water, power and shelter.
snippet: This author's message to disaster risk management professionals is simple: treat communication as lifeline infrastructure and plan for redundancy, just as we do for water, power and shelter.

title: Comprehensive analysis of disaster events and their impacts with emphasis on water-induced disasters in Nepal
content: This study provides a comprehensive analysis of the spatiotemporal dynamics of disaster events, with a focus on water-induced disasters and their associated impacts across Nepal for the period 2011–2025.
snippet: This study provides a comprehensive analysis of the spatiotemporal dynamics of disaster events, with a focus on water-induced disasters and their associated impacts across Nepal for the period

title: Integrating education into National Adaptation Plans (NAPs) – Launch of New resources for NAP teams and education ministries
content: This webinar will showcase new resources aimed at supporting NAP teams and education ministries to effectively integrate the education sector into the NAP process, and explore emerging good practice through a dynamic panel discussion.
snippet: This webinar will showcase new resources aimed at supporting NAP teams and education ministries to effectively integrate the education sector into the NAP process, and explore emerging good practice

title: From local action to lasting impact
content: This report captures the outcomes and impact of the Urban Climate Resilience Program (UCRP) in Spain between 2023 and 2026.
snippet: This report captures the outcomes and impact of the Urban Climate Resilience Program (UCRP) in Spain between 2023 and 2026.

title: Attribution of extreme weather and climate events and their impacts (2026)
content: This report reviews the current state of extreme event attribution and the emerging field of extreme event impact attribution, examining how these approaches can improve understanding of climate risks and inform planning, policy and decision-making.
snippet: This report reviews the current state of extreme event attribution and the emerging field of extreme event impact attribution, examining how these approaches can improve understanding of climate risks

title: As climate extremes collide, attribution science evolves
content: An assessment says climate attribution science has moved beyond asking if human-caused global warming is driving deadly heat waves, floods and wildfires. The focus is now on how severe future impacts will become as extreme events increasingly overlap
snippet: An assessment says climate attribution science has moved beyond asking if human-caused global warming is driving deadly heat waves, floods and wildfires. The focus is now on how severe future impacts

title: As climate change expands mosquito ranges, better monitoring is key to preventing disease
content: Monitoring is expensive and labor intensive. But it helps public health officials stop outbreaks.
snippet: Monitoring is expensive and labor intensive. But it helps public health officials stop outbreaks.

title: Professional development program - Workshop 2: Disaster recovery for all
content: This interactive workshop will focus on bridging research and practice in disaster recovery.
snippet: This interactive workshop will focus on bridging research and practice in disaster recovery.

title: Professional Development Program - Workshop 1: Unpacking disaster risk reduction: Building practical approaches to identify and reduce risks
content: The workshop will explore core disaster risk reduction concepts and introduce practical tools that can be applied across different contexts.
snippet: The workshop will explore core disaster risk reduction concepts and introduce practical tools that can be applied across different contexts.

title: Transforming our disaster risk lens – From response to prevention
content: The course introduces the foundations of disaster risk management (DRM) and explores how risk considerations can be systematically integrated into development planning and decision-making.
snippet: The course introduces the foundations of disaster risk management (DRM) and explores how risk considerations can be systematically integrated into development planning and decision-making.

title: Participatory climate risk assessment manual: Cameroon and Central Africa Republic
content: This manual aims to strengthen the capacity of facilitators, local authorities and community stakeholders to conduct participatory climate risk assessments and develop locally led climate adaptation actions.
snippet: This manual aims to strengthen the capacity of facilitators, local authorities and community stakeholders to conduct participatory climate risk assessments and develop locally led climate adaptation

title: Spain wildfires: with 13 dead, we need to start measuring fire damage in lives – not just hectares
content: Forest fires will continue, meaning we need ongoing investment in prevention, land management and firefighting resources. But we also need to pay more attention to human behaviour.
snippet: Forest fires will continue, meaning we need ongoing investment in prevention, land management and firefighting resources. But we also need to pay more attention to human behaviour.

title: They were offered free flood protection. So why are so few homeowners saying yes?
content: A government program elevates some Louisiana homes at no cost, but skepticism and unanswered questions are slowing participation.
snippet: A government program elevates some Louisiana homes at no cost, but skepticism and unanswered questions are slowing participation.

title: Fiji stages its first full-scale night-time airport crash simulation
content: Firefighters, military personnel, police officers and medics mobilised to a simulated aircraft crash on 26 June 2026, as Fiji Airports Limited conducted its first-ever large-scale night-time airport emergency exercise.
snippet: Firefighters, military personnel, police officers and medics mobilised to a simulated aircraft crash on 26 June 2026, as Fiji Airports Limited conducted its first-ever large-scale night-time airport

title: Heat waves affect all types of power generation, not just nuclear
content: Extreme heatwaves increasingly force power plants to reduce output, a problem often misattributed solely to nuclear safety. In reality, nuclear curtailment stems from environmental regulations preventing thermal pollution and reduced cooling efficiency.
snippet: Extreme heatwaves increasingly force power plants to reduce output, a problem often misattributed solely to nuclear safety. In reality, nuclear curtailment stems from environmental regulations

title: Program Coordinator (Consultant)
content: The position will provide technical, operational, and administrative support to ensure the timely delivery of programme activities and outputs, including activities implemented through the Asian Disaster Readiness Fund (ADRF).
snippet: The position will provide technical, operational, and administrative support to ensure the timely delivery of programme activities and outputs, including activities implemented through the Asian

title: "ADPC’s Young Professional Platform - Interns Talk” 8th Episode on "Beyond hazards: uncovering hidden vulnerabilities in disaster risk"
content: This webinar will explore how integrating geospatial perspectives with social vulnerability analysis can provide a more comprehensive understanding of disaster risk.
snippet: This webinar will explore how integrating geospatial perspectives with social vulnerability analysis can provide a more comprehensive understanding of disaster risk.

title: Recalibrating risk: A simplified model for North Atlantic hurricanes in a warming climate
content: In this study, the authors introduce the Schroders Capital Insurance-Linked Securities (SCILS) model that leverages projected climate proxies to estimate category-specific North Atlantic landfall rate changes for different global warming levels.
snippet: In this study, the authors introduce the Schroders Capital Insurance-Linked Securities (SCILS) model that leverages projected climate proxies to estimate category-specific North Atlantic landfall rate

title: Indigenous knowledge and community disaster resilience to cyclones: Evidence from the South-Central coastal region of Bangladesh
content: The main aim of the study is to identify, analyze, document, and improve indigenous knowledge and local practices for managing cyclone and storm-surge risks.
snippet: The main aim of the study is to identify, analyze, document, and improve indigenous knowledge and local practices for managing cyclone and storm-surge risks.

title: Climate change is costing people sleep
content: Sufficient sleep quality and duration are essential for people’s health and well-being, but hotter nighttime temperatures are making it harder for people to get the rest they need.
snippet: Sufficient sleep quality and duration are essential for people’s health and well-being, but hotter nighttime temperatures are making it harder for people to get the rest they need.

title: Access to disaster risk financing for frontline local actors
content: This report shows how disaster risk financing reaches – or fails to reach – frontline actors, and identifies practical ways to close the gaps that keep timely, adequate, locally led financing out of reach.
snippet: This report shows how disaster risk financing reaches – or fails to reach – frontline actors, and identifies practical ways to close the gaps that keep timely, adequate, locally led financing out of

title: Global study reveals how heat stress is intensifying worldwide
content: Using the Universal Thermal Climate Index (UTCI), the study shows that hazardous heat is becoming more frequent, more intense and more widespread, with hotter nights, longer heat stress seasons and increasing numbers of people exposed to hazardous heat.
snippet: Using the Universal Thermal Climate Index (UTCI), the study shows that hazardous heat is becoming more frequent, more intense and more widespread, with hotter nights, longer heat stress seasons and

title: Standard operating procedures: Early action to El Niño/La Niña episodes
content: The following Standard Operating Procedures are intended to help catalyse and guide earlier humanitarian and development action to future ENSO-related extreme weather events.
snippet: The following Standard Operating Procedures are intended to help catalyse and guide earlier humanitarian and development action to future ENSO-related extreme weather events.

title: Can humanity regain control over fire?
content: For millennia, fire was humanity's greatest tool. Today, it’s one of our greatest threats. Recovering control will require learning from the past while confronting the political choices of the present.
snippet: For millennia, fire was humanity's greatest tool. Today, it’s one of our greatest threats. Recovering control will require learning from the past while confronting the political choices of the

title: Reflective paint can keep your home cooler
content: A new paint job on your house could be an opportunity to make your home look – and feel – a little cooler.
snippet: A new paint job on your house could be an opportunity to make your home look – and feel – a little cooler.

title: First Nations women, cultural fire knowledge, wellbeing and memory
content: This report reveals that supporting First Nations women in fire and land management is a critical, yet untapped, strategy for protecting Australian communities from escalating climate disasters.
snippet: This report reveals that supporting First Nations women in fire and land management is a critical, yet untapped, strategy for protecting Australian communities from escalating climate disasters.

title: Associate Programme Management Officer, P2
content: The Associate Programme Management Officer for IRP is located in UNDRR Office in Kobe, Japan, and reports to the Programme Management Officer, Head of Recovery Readiness Unit, UNDRR Office in Geneva.
snippet: The Associate Programme Management Officer for IRP is located in UNDRR Office in Kobe, Japan, and reports to the Programme Management Officer, Head of Recovery Readiness Unit, UNDRR Office in Geneva.

title: How often will 100-year floods occur in future?
content: Heavy precipitation becomes more intense with every degree the Earth warms. This has an impact on flooding.
snippet: Heavy precipitation becomes more intense with every degree the Earth warms. This has an impact on flooding.

title: Moving towards transilience: Rethinking the role of flood early warning systems in a changing world
content: This paper reviews resilience as the capacity to “bounce back” and introduce transilience as the capacity to “bounce forward” in the context of disaster risk reduction.
snippet: This paper reviews resilience as the capacity to “bounce back” and introduce transilience as the capacity to “bounce forward” in the context of disaster risk reduction.

title: Operationalizing the Fire Warning: distinguishing structural constraints from training-sensitive perceptions
content: This research evaluates the operational utility of Fire Warnings—tactical National Weather Service (NWS) alerts issued at the request of local emergency management agencies—by examining how exposure and training influence practitioner adoption.
snippet: This research evaluates the operational utility of Fire Warnings—tactical National Weather Service (NWS) alerts issued at the request of local emergency management agencies—by examining how exposure

title: Early Warnings for All will fail without local power
content: Early warning for all is not achievable without local power. The choice facing policymakers is clear: reinforce centralised systems that fail the most at-risk, or invest in locally led solutions that save lives, livelihoods, and dignity.
snippet: Early warning for all is not achievable without local power. The choice facing policymakers is clear: reinforce centralised systems that fail the most at-risk, or invest in locally led solutions that

title: State of the UK Climate in 2025
content: This report provides a summary of the state of the UK's climate in 2025
snippet: This report provides a summary of the state of the UK's climate in 2025

title: This could be the strongest El Niño on record
content: Already, tropical cyclones are peppering the Pacific and skipping the Atlantic, and U.S. temperatures are topsy-turvy.
snippet: Already, tropical cyclones are peppering the Pacific and skipping the Atlantic, and U.S. temperatures are topsy-turvy.

title: A dynamic multilevel behavioural framework for emergency preparedness
content: Using a pandemic as central illustrative example of an emergency this paper merges the emergency management cycle with the socio-ecological model and offers a behavioural framework that captures the complex temporal and social dimensions of preparedness.
snippet: Using a pandemic as central illustrative example of an emergency this paper merges the emergency management cycle with the socio-ecological model and offers a behavioural framework that captures the

title: Living with fire: Why the Pan-Amazon needs prevention, not just suppression
content: As El Niño intensifies, evidence from across the Amazon basin shows why Amazon wildfire prevention depends on Indigenous knowledge and fire governance built from the territories up.
snippet: As El Niño intensifies, evidence from across the Amazon basin shows why Amazon wildfire prevention depends on Indigenous knowledge and fire governance built from the territories up.

title: Forest news
content: Forests News is a platform for news and analysis on forests, landscapes, and nature-based solutions
snippet: Forests News is a platform for news and analysis on forests, landscapes, and nature-based solutions

title: Climate disinformation is already harming Europeans. Policymakers should protect us
content: This briefing warns that climate disinformation is spreading through European media and online platforms, undermining public trust, distorting debate on climate action and highlighting the need for stronger policy and regulatory responses.
